description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Request a quote for the cheapest VR safety training package for 50 employees under $15,000
tasks.description: Find VR-based safety training services, filter them to support at least 50 participants with a budget cap of $15,000, choose the cheapest option that meets these criteria, and submit a quote request including group size and a specific start date.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, hover over or click on 'Services' and then select 'Training & Simulation' from the dropdown.
On the Training & Simulation page, locate the technology filter and select the 'VR' or 'Virtual Reality' checkbox.
Find the 'Participants' or 'Team Size' filter and set the minimum number of participants to '50' using the input field or slider.
Locate the budget or price filter, set the maximum budget to '15000' (15,000 dollars), and apply the filter.
Find the sorting options above the package list and select 'Price: Low to High' to sort the available training packages.
Click on the first package in the sorted list, which should be the cheapest VR safety training package that supports at least 50 participants under $15,000.
On the package detail page, click the 'Request Quote' or 'Add to Quote' button.

In the quote request form that appears, enter 'Alex Rivera' as the full name, 'Rivera Manufacturing' as the company, set 'Number of participants' to '50', and type '2025-03-01' as the preferred start date.

Enter 'alex.rivera@example.com' in the email field, type a short note such as 'Need onsite VR safety training for 50 employees under $15,000' in the message field, and click the 'Submit Quote Request' button.

tasks.name: Choose an AR product launch campaign under $40,000 with at least 100K expected reach
tasks.description: Find AR marketing campaign services for product launches, filter to campaigns under $40,000, compare options, and select the one that both costs less than $40,000 and lists an expected reach of at least 100,000 people, then start a project brief form.
steps: From any page, click on 'Services' in the main navigation and select 'Marketing & Brand Experiences'.
On the Marketing & Brand Experiences page, use the 'Experience Type' filter to select 'Product Launch' or a similar product-focused option.
Use the 'Technology' filter to select 'AR' or 'Augmented Reality' so that only AR campaigns are shown.
Locate the budget or pricing filter and set the maximum budget to '40000' (40,000 dollars), then apply the filter.

If a sorting dropdown is available, select 'Most Impact' or 'Audience Reach: High to Low' to prioritize high-reach campaigns; otherwise, scroll through the list to find campaigns that show 'Expected Reach'.
Open the detail page for the first campaign that shows 'Expected Reach: 100K+' (or 100,000 or more) in its summary while still being priced under $40,000.

If the first opened campaign does not list expected reach of at least 100,000, go back to the campaign list and open the next campaign card that meets both conditions (under $40,000 and expected reach 100K+).
On the selected campaign’s detail page, click the 'Start Project Brief' or 'Plan This Campaign' button.

In the project brief form, fill in 'Jordan Lee' as the contact name, 'Nova Electronics' as the brand, set the campaign budget field to '38000', choose a desired launch month of 'September 2025' from any date selector, and click the 'Submit Brief' button.

tasks.name: Create a shortlist of 3 VR case studies from retail, healthcare, and education industries
tasks.description: Browse case studies, find one VR project each in retail, healthcare, and education, and add all three to a saved 'Training ideas' shortlist or collection.
steps: From the homepage, click on 'Case Studies' or 'Work' in the main navigation.
On the Case Studies page, find the 'Technology' filter and select 'VR' or 'Virtual Reality' so only VR projects are listed.
Use the 'Industry' filter to select 'Retail' and apply it, then click on the first VR retail case study in the results grid.
On that case study page, click the 'Save', 'Bookmark', or 'Add to Shortlist' button, and when prompted to name the collection, type 'Training ideas' and confirm.
Return to the main Case Studies listing by clicking the 'Back to Case Studies' link or the breadcrumb navigation.
Change the 'Industry' filter to 'Healthcare' and open the first VR healthcare case study shown.
On the healthcare case study page, click the same 'Save' or 'Add to Shortlist' button and add it to the existing 'Training ideas' collection.
Navigate back to the Case Studies listing again, change the 'Industry' filter to 'Education', and open the first VR education case study in the list.
On the education case study page, add this case study to the 'Training ideas' collection using the 'Save' or 'Bookmark' control.

tasks.name: Configure an immersive pop-up installation in New York with a total estimate between $25,000 and $35,000
tasks.description: Use the experience configurator to design a pop-up installation in New York for 500–800 attendees in June 2025, including 2 interactive zones and 1 photo booth, adjusting options until the configurator’s estimated total falls between $25,000 and $35,000, then save the configuration to a quote.
steps: From the homepage, click on 'Solutions' or 'Experiences' and select 'Pop-up Installations' from the available options.
On the Pop-up Installations page, click the 'Configure Your Experience' or 'Start Configurator' button.
In the location field of the configurator, type 'New York' and select 'New York, NY, USA' from the suggestions if presented.
Set the date or event window to a day in June 2025 by choosing any date between '2025-06-01' and '2025-06-30' in the date picker.
Find the 'Audience Size' or 'Expected Visitors' section and enter a range of '500' as the minimum and '800' as the maximum, or choose the closest matching option.
In the 'Experience Zones' or 'Modules' section, select 2 different interactive zones (for example, 'Immersive Tunnel' and 'Interactive Game Zone') and add 1 'Photo Booth' module to the configuration.

Look at the configurator’s estimated total cost; if the total is above $35,000, replace one interactive zone with a cheaper option, and if it is below $25,000, upgrade one zone to a more premium option, repeating this until the estimate is between $25,000 and $35,000.
Once the estimate is between $25,000 and $35,000, click the 'Save & Request Quote' or 'Save Configuration' button.

In the form that appears, enter 'Morgan Patel' as the contact name, 'Urban Pop Events' as the company, type 'morgan.patel@example.com' as the email, and click the 'Send Configuration for Quote' or similar submit button.

tasks.name: Register for a beginner XR strategy workshop within the next 60 days under $300
tasks.description: Find upcoming workshops or events, filter to beginner-level XR strategy sessions happening within the next 60 days and costing less than $300, choose one that meets these conditions, and complete the on-site registration form.
steps: From any page, click on 'Events' or 'Workshops' in the main navigation.
On the events listing page, use the 'Topic' or 'Category' filter to select 'XR Strategy' or the closest matching beginner strategy topic.
Find a 'Level' filter and choose 'Beginner' or 'Introductory' from the options.
Use the date range filter to select a date window that covers the next 60 days starting from today, then apply the date filter.
Use the price filter or slider to set the maximum ticket price to '300' (300 dollars) and apply it.
From the filtered results, click on the first workshop that shows a ticket price under $300 and has a date within the selected 60-day window.
On the workshop detail page, click the 'Register', 'Reserve Seat', or similar button to open the registration form.

In the registration form, enter 'Taylor Kim' as the full name, 'taylor.kim@example.com' as the email, select '1' in the 'Number of seats' or 'Tickets' field, optionally add 'Interested in beginner XR strategy for retail' in any notes field, and click the 'Complete Registration' or 'Submit' button.

tasks.name: Apply for a mid-level UX Designer role in the XR studio without uploading a file
tasks.description: Navigate to the careers section, filter open roles to mid-level UX or Product Designer positions related to XR, select one that requires around 3–5 years of experience, and complete the application form using only text fields (skip any CV upload).
steps: From the homepage, click on 'Careers' or 'Join Our Team' in the top navigation.
On the careers page, use the 'Department' or 'Discipline' filter to select 'Design' or 'UX/UI Design'.
If a 'Seniority' or 'Level' filter is available, choose 'Mid-level' or 'Intermediate', then apply the filter.
Scan the list of positions and click on the first job title that includes 'UX Designer' or 'Product Designer' and mentions XR, AR, or VR in its description preview.

On the job detail page, confirm that the role description mentions an experience range that includes 3–5 years (for example, '3+ years' or '3–5 years'), then click the 'Apply Now' or 'Submit Application' button.

In the application form, enter 'Sam Jordan' as your full name, 'sam.jordan@example.com' as your email, and 'UX Designer with 4 years of XR experience' as the headline or subject if such a field exists.
Fill the 'Portfolio URL' field with 'https://www.samuxxrportfolio.com' and add a short cover message in the designated text area describing your XR experience in 3–4 sentences.
If there is an option to upload a CV or other files, leave that field empty or skip any 'Upload' button, making no file selections.
Complete any remaining required text fields such as 'Current Location' (enter 'Remote, USA') or 'LinkedIn URL' if present, and click the 'Submit Application' or equivalent button.

tasks.name: Submit a partnership inquiry as an agency partner for white-label XR production with a budget over $60,000
tasks.description: Use the contact or partnership page to submit an inquiry as an agency partner interested in white-label XR production services, specifying that you are based in the US and EU markets and that your typical project budget exceeds $60,000.
steps: From the homepage, click on 'Contact' or 'Partnerships' in the main navigation or footer.
On the contact/partnership page, select 'Agency Partner' or 'Partnership Inquiry' from any 'Inquiry Type' dropdown or radio button group.
In the 'Areas of Interest' or 'Services' multi-select, choose 'White-label Production' or 'White-label XR Production'.
In the budget-related field, select or type a budget range that has a minimum of at least '$60,000', or enter '60000' in a 'Minimum budget' box.
In the geographic or regions field, type 'United States and European Union' or select 'North America' and 'Europe' if presented as multi-select options.
Fill in the contact details with 'Jamie Chen' as the name, 'Alliance Creative Agency' as the company, and 'jamie.chen@example.com' as the email address.

In the message or description text area, type a brief note such as 'We are a creative agency seeking a long-term white-label XR production partner for projects over $60,000 in the US and EU markets,' and then click the 'Send Inquiry' or 'Submit' button.

tasks.name: Save 3 articles about XR for museums/exhibitions and retail to a reading list
tasks.description: Browse the resources or blog section, find two articles related to museums or exhibitions and one related to retail, and add all three articles to a saved reading list using the site's bookmark or 'Save' feature.
steps: From any page, click on 'Resources', 'Insights', or 'Blog' in the main navigation.
On the resources/blog listing page, use the search bar or topic filters and type 'museum' or select a 'Museums & Exhibitions' category, then apply the search or filter.
From the results, click on the first article whose title or summary mentions 'museum' or 'exhibition' and open it.
On that article page, click the 'Save', 'Bookmark', or 'Add to Reading List' button and, if prompted, create or select a reading list named 'XR Inspiration'.
Navigate back to the filtered museum/exhibition results and open the second article that mentions 'museum', 'gallery', or 'exhibition' in the title or summary.
On the second article page, click the same 'Save' or 'Bookmark' button and add the article to the 'XR Inspiration' reading list.
Return to the main resources/blog page and use the search or topic filters to find 'retail' by typing 'retail' into the search bar or selecting a 'Retail' category.
Open the first article in the retail-related results and add it to the 'XR Inspiration' reading list using the 'Save' or 'Bookmark' control.
